# THE SIGNAL
> A public, self-grading oracle that makes dated, falsifiable tech predictions every day and keeps score in the open. Rules-based, no LLM.

## Pages
- Homepage: /
- The receipts (dated calls, graded): /receipts.html
- The arena (bet against the machine): /arena.html
- Sleep mode (the oracle dreams, always running): /sleep.html
- The bloodline (the breeding population of strategies): /bloodline.html
- The manifold (the prediction core + the algorithm benchmark): /manifold.html
- The event horizon (the reversals the machine is calling): /horizon.html
- Open dataset: /dataset/
- Today's call (plain text): /cli
- RSS feed: /feed.xml
- Sitemap: /sitemap.xml
- Latest call: /call/394.html

## API for agents
Static JSON, read-only, CORS-open. No key, no signup.
- Discovery: /api/oracle.json
- Today's calls: /api/today.json
- Full record + calibration: /api/record.json
- Observatory (sectors, fear/greed, chasm watch): /api/observatory.json
- Benchmark (the manifold vs momentum, PageRank and others): /api/benchmark.json
- Event horizon (topics the manifold calls peaking/bottoming): /api/horizon.json
- OpenAPI: /openapi.json
- Agent manifest: /.well-known/ai-plugin.json
- MCP resources: /.well-known/mcp.json
Agents can place stateless bets; see the how_to_bet field in oracle.json.

## How it works
Reads ten public sources from technical to general: arXiv, GitHub, crates.io, Lobsters, Hacker News, dev.to, Reddit, Ars Technica, Google News and Wikipedia pageviews. It keeps a growing daily corpus, tracks each term's velocity and diffusion down the funnel (a CHASM bet fires when a term leaves the dev bubble for the general public), grades its own calibration (Brier score) and reweights its sources by realized hit rate. Each call carries a concrete win condition and is settled HIT or MISS against later signals. Current record: 61-2. Tech Acceleration Index today: 66 (SURGING).
